attacking_style: Launches attacks in final 1-2km on small rises or final kilometer of climbs, but repeatedly cracks when countered or lacks finishing punch to sustain efforts through the line.
team_tactics: Primary role is tempo-setting domestique for team leaders (Roglič), deployed to control climbs early and police breakaway threats rather than personal ambitions. Team relies on his pacing but doesn't protect his positioning late.
terrain_preference: Short punchy climbs where he can set tempo or launch late attacks (Madonna del Ghisallo, Mont Brouilly). Struggles on technical descents at speed and when altitude/sustained power is required.
vulnerability: Exposed on high-speed technical descents (crashed twice at Tour 2019, Vuelta crash). Psychological fragility when countered—checks computer mid-effort, immediately cracks when attacked. Poor late-race positioning despite domestique work.
key_patterns: Attacks final 1-2km but cracks when countered; lacked finishing power in 4+ instances
Crashes on technical descents at speed; multiple high-speed incidents in Grand Tours
Deployed as tempo-setter for Jumbo-Visma leaders, not protected for personal results
Shows psychological collapse signals: checking computer mid-attack, sitting up after counters
Poor positioning despite tempo work; finishes far behind after domestique duties
Occasional sprint speed surprise but inconsistent finishing ability for own chances
form_trajectory: negative: Recent observations show decline from 2018 stage wins to pure domestique role with crashes, poor positioning, and psychological fragility under pressure by 2020-2021
